title: amaryllis × ringens
intro: Amaryllis × ringens, commonly known as the Belladonna lily, is a hybrid plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its showy, trumpet-shaped flowers and toxic foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, with its alkaloids possessing medicinal properties such as antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits associated with Amaryllis × ringens include potential treatment for respiratory issues, pain relief, and antiviral infections, although its toxicity demands caution and expert guidance. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as extracts and tinctures, can be used under medical supervision, but it is essential to note that the plant's toxicity may lead to adverse effects if mishandled.
